items.prompt: Why does Parquet help analytical queries according to Example 12.3?
choices: It stores data by column so queries can read only needed columns
It forces every query to scan every byte of every row always
It replaces all networks with USB drives
It removes the need for any compression
items.explain: Example 12.3 states Parquet is columnar with compression suited to Spark/Hadoop analytics.

items.prompt: Avro is described as a row-oriented format common in Kafka-style streaming with schema evolution.
items.explain: Example 12.4 contrasts Avro (row, streaming) with ORC (columnar, Hive warehouses).

items.prompt: Which storage type best fits unstructured media archives and lakehouse files that grow without block provisioning?
choices: Object storage in a flat namespace
Only a single local floppy disk
Only in-memory registers with no durability
items.explain: Examples 12.5 and the object-storage subsection place unstructured archives on object stores.

items.prompt: When is block storage preferred over object storage in Example 12.6?
items.rubric: Mention low-latency / transactional databases / POSIX-like I/O on VMs.
items.sample_answer: For low-latency transactional databases needing POSIX-like I/O on attached volumes.

items.prompt: ORC is described as a columnar format optimized for Hive and other read-heavy Hadoop warehouses.
items.explain: Example 12.4 positions ORC for Hive-style read-heavy warehouses.
